title: Two Pointers
one_liner: When the array is sorted (or you need to find a pair), scan with two indices instead of nested loops.

title: Sliding Window
one_liner: When the question asks about a contiguous subarray with a constraint, slide a window instead of nested loops.

title: Binary Search
one_liner: When the input is sorted (or you can binary-search on the answer), halve the search space each step.

title: Prefix Sum
one_liner: When you'll query the sum of many subarrays, precompute prefix sums once and answer each query in O(1).

title: Fast & Slow Pointers
one_liner: When detecting cycles or finding the middle of a sequence, move two pointers at different speeds.

title: Backtracking
one_liner: When you need all subsets, permutations, or combinations, build solutions recursively and undo failed choices.

title: Hash Map
one_liner: When brute force is O(n²) with a nested lookup loop, a hash map collapses it to O(n).

title: Kadane's Algorithm
one_liner: For maximum subarray sum, track best-ending-here vs best-so-far in a single pass.
